scope: cross-cutting P0 gate across WP0, WP2, WP4, and WP7
purpose: Guarantee that every playable release has responsive controls, a complete accepted asset production set bound to real game targets, and smooth deterministic simulation with replay evidence.

forbidden: maintain a transform separate from the rendered canvas

owns: declaration of stable visual slots on gameplay objects

forbidden: declare that a release target visibly uses an asset

owns: one work item per required slot
forbidden: accept a production set with missing work items
place a world-role asset on a UI layer
report success without runtime target evidence
derive cadence from a Golden id
couple local cadence to network defaults
use wall-clock pointer duration as replay truth
skip a simulation tick in the replay artifact
treat file existence as visible binding evidence
treat a semantic playtest as physical input or rendering evidence

viewportContract.logicalSpace: The game design resolution declared by the runtime manifest.
viewportContract.canvasContentSpace: The visible game rectangle after aspect-ratio scaling and letterboxing.
viewportContract.cssSpace: Browser CSS pixels returned by the game canvas content rectangle.
viewportContract.deviceSpace: Physical pixels derived only for rendering quality, never for semantic placement.
canonicalTransform: Read the rendered game content rectangle from the same canvas renderer used by GDJS.
Project host and env safe-area insets into that content rectangle.
Resolve semantic anchor and fractional offsets inside the safe content rectangle.
Resolve size from the content short edge, then clamp to declared minimum and maximum CSS pixels.
Position every control inside one overlay root whose bounds exactly match the canvas content rectangle.

offsetPolicy.rule: Offsets are relative to the selected anchor and must not become project-authored CSS pixels.

invariants: controls remain fully inside the safe canvas content rectangle
canvas letterboxing does not change semantic control position
orientation changes do not duplicate controls or event listeners
pointer hit geometry equals visible control geometry
control layout is recomputable from UIControlLayoutSpec and UIViewportSnapshot

assetBindingContract.slotIdentity: targetVisualSlotId is the only runtime binding target. Free-form bindingTarget strings are stale input and must fail closed.

bindingModes.object-resource: Install the accepted resource on the declared target object renderer.
bindingModes.attached-visual: Attach a rendering-only visual to the declared gameplay object and prove transform following while the gameplay object retains behavior and collision ownership.
bindingModes.layer-background: Install the resource on the declared scene layer background slot.
bindingModes.ui-slot: Install the resource only in a declared UI visual slot.

assetBindingContract.worldRoleRule: player, enemy, collectible, and platform assets may not use ui-slot or an untracked free-standing overlay.
playableBindingChecks: declared target renders the accepted revision
browser screenshot region contains the bound visual
assetBindingContract.requiredSlotPolicy: A required generated slot blocks playable and publishable states until both asset acceptance and playable binding acceptance pass.
assetBindingContract.optionalSlotPolicy: An optional slot may use an explicit placeholder debt but may not be reported as generated-and-bound success.
forbidden: world asset rendered on UI layer
generated image accepted without semantic visual review

assetProductionContract.rule: A template-required production set must create one independent work item per required visual slot, run every item through the conditional observe-diagnose-act-reobserve loop, accept every final revision, and prove complete targetVisualSlotId coverage before runtime binding.
assetProductionContract.minimumLoop: resolve-or-generate, vision inspection, deterministic validation and final review are the shortest valid path; segmentation, masked repair, color application and normalization are selected only from typed defects.
assetProductionContract.playableVersionRule: No new or continued ProjectVersion is playable or committable until all required assets are accepted, bound and included in aggregate runtime evidence.
forbidden: runtime hot swap of intermediate revisions
partial production set counted as playable

clockSeparation.input: Input sampling into tick-numbered intent frames.
clockSeparation.network: Input, command, or snapshot transport cadence.
clockSeparation.render: requestAnimationFrame presentation with interpolation when state cadence is lower.

local-interactive.interpolation: optional when render and simulation are phase-aligned

realtime-lockstep.interpolation: required when render Hz exceeds committed simulation Hz

tickPolicyContract.overloadRule: Report performance debt after the catch-up budget; never permanently lower a release below the profile minimum.
tickPolicyContract.replayRule: Record one canonical frame for every simulation tick, including empty input frames. Replay drives the same adapter and simulation step without DOM or wall-clock input.
forbidden: wall-clock tap timer as replay truth
network profile silently applied to local mode
fake interpolation without previous and next committed states

aggregateAcceptance.decision: A release is playable only when every required viewport, asset-binding, tick-performance, replay, and browser-playtest check passes.
assetGates: every required production set has AssetProductionSetAcceptanceReceipt
all required bindings have runtime receipts
browserGates: player input changes real gameplay state
bound asset appears on target object
aggregateAcceptance.goldenRule: A Golden run may prove provider and pipeline execution only after PlayableRuntimeEvidence passes; PNG existence, binding manifest existence, or semantic playtest alone can never mark it passed.

migration.strategy: replace in place with no compatibility aliases or dual runtime
deleteOrReject: fixed body-relative control left/top emitted from semantic placement
free-form AssetSpec.bindingTarget as runtime target truth
generic GameCastleAsset UI overlay injection for world-role assets
mixed-role composite parent sheet generation and fixed-grid prompt separation
partial asset production set used as a playable project version
a separate standard GDevelop game loop for projects that declare interactive Tick input
Golden-only asset or input binding paths
playable success based only on files, manifests, or semantic pseudo-playtest
migration.artifactVersioning: New artifacts use schemaVersion 1 under this contract. Stale shapes fail closed; no reader accepts both stale and canonical fields.

terraImplementationOrder: PRT-0 add contract validators and fail-closed artifact schemas without changing runtime behavior
PRT-1 implement TickPolicyResolver, 60 Hz local simulation, >=30 Hz realtime policies, clock separation, replay and performance evidence
PRT-2 implement RuntimeViewportCoordinator and migrate all runtime controls to one canvas-aligned overlay transform
PRT-3 implement AssetProductionPlanner and the conditional generate-observe-diagnose-act-reobserve loop defined by shared/asset-production-pipeline-contract.json
PRT-4 add module VisualSlotDeclaration, migrate AssetSpec to targetVisualSlotId, implement RuntimeAssetBinder and delete generic world-asset overlay injection
PRT-5 extend RuntimeValidator and release assembly with aggregate PlayableRuntimeEvidence including assetProductionReport
PRT-6 rebuild Golden-001 from a clean project and pass full asset-production, viewport, target binding, Tick performance, replay, and real browser gates
terraStopConditions: Do not continue past PRT-1 if local observed simulation cadence is below 60 Hz or realtime policy permits below 30 Hz.
Do not continue past PRT-2 if any runtime control still uses body-relative fixed coordinates.
Do not continue past PRT-3 if any required work item can bypass reobservation after pixel changes, or a production set can pass with missing acceptance or target visual slot coverage.
Do not continue past PRT-4 if a required world-role asset can pass while rendered as a detached overlay.
Do not declare completion until the clean Golden release passes aggregate browser evidence.
definitionOfDone: machine contract and human architecture agree
old paths listed by migration are deleted or fail closed
all required viewport cases pass on the released artifact
all required generated assets visibly bind to declared game targets
all required production sets prove work item plans, conditional loop histories, immutable revision lineage, per-item acceptance, and complete target visual slot coverage
local runs at 60 Hz and realtime policies never fall below 30 Hz
replay reproduces the final state hash
writer, independent tester, and independent auditor evidence are recorded
status remains designed until all evidence exists
